Convex Champions
================

Champions use Convex to build fast, reliable, and dynamic apps. They support our thriving community through product feedback, design suggestions, and content.

Meet the Champions
------------------

![Photo of Abhishek](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fabhishek.38d8200d.jpg&w=1536&q=75)

Abhishek is an experienced front-end developer and is currently an indie hacker and a freelancer based in India. Who is working on an influencer automation platform with Convex and Nextjs.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

I am interested in automation, and with the help of AI, it has become much easier to achieve. My goal is to develop an AI-powered application that can automate the work of a ghostwriter, eliminating the need for human writers. Additionally, I am currently working on an app called "Present," which aims to automate influencer marketing. You can expect to see the launch of this app soon.

![Photo of Bobby Alv](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fbobby.dbadff8c.png&w=1536&q=75)

I'm a software engineer from Texas. Currently working on Relio, a commercial real estate CRM, and other side projects exploring Convex as much as I can.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

I'd love to build more components for Convex as well as a few gadgets to help with development. As a personal project I'd love to build a customized project tracker with email capabilities.

![Photo of Eric Li](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Feric.0659b78a.jpg&w=1536&q=75)

Often building projects that analyze data in my life or save me a few minutes every day. See more at [erichli.com](https://erichli.com/)
.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

Ask Google Reviews in natural language.

![Photo of Eva Decker](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Feva.c34b147a.jpg&w=1536&q=75)

Eva is a design engineer who enjoys building useful web-based tools for people.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

Right now it's the app I'm building, Namesake! In the U.S., it's difficult, costly, and time-consuming to change legal names and gender markers. There's a real opportunity to use technology to streamline the process with more accessible guides, forms, and user-contributed tips—that's exactly what I'm hoping to do with Namesake. Convex has been a great help!

![Photo of Gorka](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fgorka.bce4abe9.png&w=1536&q=75)

Web developer React/Rescript. Worked at 4 startups in California and now I build the software infrastructure for 3 businesses in the construction industry. Bullish on Convex.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

"An intuitive, full-blown modern ERP/CRM/Product Configurator for Window manufacturing that will be connected to CNC machines, cutter, and other workflows."

![Photo of Hamza Saleem](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fhamza.eeb4d612.png&w=1536&q=75)

I'm a developer who loves diving into cool new projects, especially ones that mix creativity and tech. I’m all about experimenting with AI and finding ways to make life easier (and more fun) through code. Outside of work, I’m probably geeking out over the latest tech or brainstorming my next big idea.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

An app that turns your wildest daydreams into reality. Imagine describing your dream—whether it's flying through the clouds, exploring a hidden city underwater, or hanging out with your favorite fictional characters—and the app uses VR and AI to create an immersive experience that makes it feel real. Because who wouldn’t want to step into their dreams every now and then?

![Photo of Hardik Verma](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fhardik.72dc3124.jpg&w=1536&q=75)

Hardik Verma is a seasoned senior software engineer with over 9 years of experience. He specializes in crafting scalable solutions with a strong focus on architecture and design patterns. Passionate about technology, he enjoys solving complex problems, mentoring developers, and sharing insights on Convex and other cutting-edge technologies. As a Convex Champion, Hardik is dedicated to fostering innovation, empowering the developer community, and driving impactful contributions to the tech ecosystem.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

One of my favorite app ideas is TravelPlannerAI, a tool that helps users plan their trips effortlessly. People from around the world have already reached out for collaboration , and nearly 800 travel plans have been created in just the past few months. I’m currently working on expanding the app by adding multi-agent workflows to automate tasks and a community feature to allow users to share their plans and experiences, further enhancing the app’s interactive and collaborative nature.

![Photo of Hosna Qasmei](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fhosna.ed8cb551.jpg&w=1536&q=75)

Hosna is a software engineer based in Los Angeles, California. She's working on ProjectPlannerAI, a platform that helps developers keep their side projects on track. Other than building side projects, she also makes YouTube videos about web & mobile development.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

I'd love an app that quizzes me about my friends and family. It would be cool to see how well I really know them and learn new stuff, making our bonds even stronger.

![Photo of Jashwanth Peddisetty](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fjashwanth.ba95998c.png&w=1536&q=75)

I am a developer passionate about creating open-source and free solutions. I enjoy gamifying processes to make them engaging and fun. Rather than being called a software developer, I prefer the title of an artist who transforms imagination and creativity into products. I love experimenting, learning new technologies, and building gamified experiences. Currently working on a interesting product to make content writing simple and more human with Convex.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

Multimodal and multi-device private AI that has knowledge of all our experiences and helps us with our tasks like J.A.R.V.I.S.

![Photo of Julián David](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fjulian.8956103f.jpg&w=1536&q=75)

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

I’ve always dreamed of building an app that grows into something impactful. One project I’d love to see come to life is Conjuntify, my indie-hacking initiative. It’s an app designed to help people living in condos manage their day-to-day activities, payments, and notices more efficiently.

![Photo of Justin S Barrett](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fjustin.aa872b86.png&w=1536&q=75)

Self-taught web developer, occasional voice actor, former CG animator. Currently, the solo dev at a growing service business near Atlanta, GA.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

The one I’m currently building for myself. Basically a multi-purpose app to track/manage/organize various things in my life. Leaning into the "personal" part of "personal productivity" instead of trusting anyone else’s system to work with my brain.

![Photo of Kamal Panara](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fkamal.14c540e8.png&w=1536&q=75)

I'm an app developer specializing in Flutter for mobile and React for web. My journey began as an indie hacker and freelancer, and today I run my own app development studio, Panara Studio.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

My dream app to build is a modern POS system. So many are terribly designed, and I think a truly intuitive one could be a game-changer for small businesses.

![Photo of MapleLeaf](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fmaple.abcc1cdc.png&w=1536&q=75)

10+ year hobbyist software dev with 5 years in the professional space, mainly focused on web dev and React, currently on the Astro UI team. Anime lover, rhythm gamer, TTRPG nerd, and sometimes [music producer](https://soundcloud.com/itsmapleleaf)
.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

"It feels like all of the good app ideas have already been built, but more games in the web space would be cool."

![Photo of Matt Luo](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fmatt.89101586.jpg&w=1536&q=75)

Matt Luo founded [ClarityText](https://www.claritytext.com/)
, a messaging app for business communities. He was previously a software architect at Goldman Sachs.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

I've always wanted to see an accounting education app built. In America, the most popular college major is business, but accounting education has barely changed for decades. Students still seem to use lectures and textbooks to decipher how new data mutates other data. A student should be able to use software to visualize how, say, depreciation flows through the various financial statements. Software brings a lot to the table: interactivity, branching, multiple cursors, form validation, and of course, an LLM to reason about why you misunderstood something the way you did.

![Photo of Mikael](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fmikael.6bb75420.jpg&w=1536&q=75)

I lead the development of [Shorty](https://shorty.run/)
 and [Number Nation](https://www.numbernation.com/)
, leveraging my experience as a founder and product expert. Shorty is backed by BootstrapLabs, a leading Silicon Valley venture capital firm focused on applied artificial intelligence. My entrepreneurial achievements include founding Witsbits, which emerged as a pioneer in cloud-managed server virtualization and was acquired by ClearCenter. We are building Shorty on Convex.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

"A hacker worthy accounting software?"

![Photo of Mohan Singh Thagunna](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fmohan.b7a1e31e.jpg&w=1536&q=75)

I’m a solution architect and software engineer specializing in iOS, Flutter, React/Next.js, and scalable backend solutions. My expertise lies in designing and building real-time applications, integrating AI-driven features, and optimizing system performance for seamless user experiences. I’m passionate about exploring innovative technologies like Convex DB to create efficient and scalable applications.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

I’ve always wanted to build an all-in-one, platform-specific solution that simplifies Convex development, making it more accessible and efficient for developers. This would include pre-built components, streamlined integrations, and powerful automation tools tailored to Convex’s capabilities.

Additionally, I’m deeply interested in AI-driven native language support, particularly through chatbots that facilitate seamless communication in local languages. Another idea I’m working on is a business-oriented, content-driven social media platform that leverages AI for personalized insights, engagement analytics, and optimized content distribution.

![Photo of Nazar](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fnazar.ec2571e3.jpeg&w=1536&q=75)

I've worked on a few projects that never got to see the day of light and as it happens, those were the ones I coded in the most (please believe me). Some of side projects that I've worked on recently and didn't document anywhere are [realtimememes.com](https://realtimememes.com/)
 and [justdj.ca](https://justdj.ca/)
. I stopped working on realtimememes.com due to my own lack of knowledge for the MVP I had/have in mind. justDJ.ca is still something I want to work on but it'll have to wait until I finish [worldshare.ca](https://www.worldshare.ca/)
.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

The application that I am currently building! I've named it Worldshare, it's an app for connecting users around the world.

![Photo of Omar](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fomar.03dbc8a2.jpg&w=1536&q=75)

Omar is a 'full stack artisan' specializing in product and brand design, with a deep understanding of technical infrastructure, market dynamics, and growth strategies. Currently, he leads Multiplicity, a new digital venture studio exploring organizational design for digital businesses in the era of AI. His contributions to the Ethereum digital art scene have garnered international attention, with features and mentions in VICE, The Atlantic, Forbes, Harper's Bazaar, Boing Boing, Esquire, Creative Bloq, and more.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

"I plan on building it on Convex! An AI agent that facilitates online debates by reinforcing intellectual rigor between contestants in real time. Say goodbye to strawmen and red herrings, and say hello to enlightenment."

![Photo of Oren Aksakal](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Foren.43449e89.jpg&w=1536&q=75)

Oren is a Lead Software Developer with over 8 years of experience leading high-performing engineering teams. Armed with a Master's in Computer Science and IT Project Management he has lead teams from small startups to large-scale enterprises like SpotOn, Acoustic (formerly IBM Marketing Cloud), and Oracle. He is currently leading a team of 6 developers at Hootsuite. Oren specializes in modern frontend technologies like React.js and TypeScript while maintaining a strong focus on mentorship and process optimization. A speaker at Infoshare 2020 and volunteer mentor with NPower Canada, he combines technical expertise with a commitment to community development, all while bringing a global perspective through his trilingual abilities in English, Turkish, and Polish.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

A collection app, merging multiple subscriptions (imagine streaming platforms, app subscriptions, gaming platforms, etc.) to one single bill with cost, usage tracking credentials sharing, easy management, etc.

![Photo of Prathamesh Gursal](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fprathamesh.1cfeb693.jpg&w=1536&q=75)

I love building things that make a real impact. For me, coding is more than just writing code—it’s about bringing ideas to life in a way that’s engaging and accessible. I see development as a mix of creativity and logic, shaping intuitive experiences that feel effortless. I enjoy making technology more fun and interactive, whether it’s gamifying experiences, simplifying workflows, or creating personalized solutions. Right now, I’m exploring ways to make digital interactions more human-centered—because great tech should work for people, not the other way around.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

"Personalized Learning Hub" – A system that curates educational content based on a user’s interests, learning pace, and goals, integrating interactive challenges, gamification, and AI-driven recommendations to enhance engagement and effectiveness.

![Photo of RJ](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Frj.037ae648.png&w=1536&q=75)

RJ is an ex-startup founder and current founding engineer at GlobeCommerce ([https://globecommerce.co](https://globecommerce.co/)
). He’s a largely self-taught programmer with experience across the stack and with a broad range of technologies and paradigms.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

"I always thought it would be neat to build a vocab-learning app which presents you with a word and its definition, and then tests you along various dimensions to reinforce your understanding of the nuances of that word's usage and connotation. Most vocab-learning apps just present you such information to learn passively, rather than drilling you on e.g. when you should use this word vs another, similar word, or teaching and then testing you on a word's etymology."

![Photo of Sara Natour](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fsara.c157d687.png&w=1536&q=75)

Sara is a full-stack web developer with a background in electrical engineering, currently based in Hannover, Germany. She specializes in building scalable, high-performance applications using JavaScript and TypeScript, with expertise in different frameworks. On the backend, she actively works with Convex to deliver real-time, dynamic solutions. Sara brings an engineering mindset to her software development work. She is currently open to new opportunities in web and IoT development throughout Germany and across Europe.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

A limitless job board/tracker to help people find and track jobs quickly, without having to worry about subscriptions.

![Photo of Shawn Erquhart](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fshawn.95bbf0a7.jpeg&w=1536&q=75)

Product engineer, worked for some startups. Done learning new frameworks, it's all Expo + Convex 'til the bots take over.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

Almost done building it, launching soon :)

![Photo of Shola Jegede](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fshola.9b1edbb2.jpg&w=1536&q=75)

Indie hacker and founding software engineer at BuildersCabal, one of Africa’s largest startup communities.

Currently building [NextMVP](https://nextmvp.tech/)
, an MVP development agency that helps ambitious founders turn their ideas into market-ready products. Most of my client projects run on Convex; it’s that efficient.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

An AI Operating System for Startup Founders, a network of AI agents working together to make building and scaling startups much easier. Last year, I built a version focused on fundraising and was among the winners of the Agent.ai AI Agent Challenge. Now, I’m exploring how to take it even further.

![Photo of Tiffany Wang](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Ftiffany.8d0a73af.png&w=1536&q=75)

Software engineer and game developer based in San Francisco using Convex to make experimental web games!

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

A simple way to make ARGs for your friends.

![Photo of Tyrese Mclean](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Ftyrese.96005ade.png&w=1536&q=75)

I'm a developer and business owner from the UK. Primarily focusing on building React apps and crafting unique experiences for enterprise and normal business owners. Got my start at 13 offering web services to local businesses, and built up from those days. I'm focused on React due to the endless possibilities it offers and recently been building some great things with Convex. We've built landing pages, design projects, mobile apps, and much more. Happy to join the team at Convex and help bridge the gap between developers and their backend choice.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

An all in one solution for developers from the UK and US to connect better, in a more tight fitted community as there is a huge premise on the internet where everything is held in the US and most developers are unable to attend.

![Photo of Varun](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fvarun.3f3efad0.jpeg&w=1536&q=75)

Varun Singh is a second-year Computer Science student at the University of Maryland-College Park, deeply engaged with the Convex platform since discovering it at a hackathon. He is the creator of HOPSCOTCH, an innovative app that aims to extend the concept of social coding events to various hobbies, leveraging Convex to enhance his development process significantly. Varun is passionate about software design, actively participates in the Convex community, and is enthusiastic about the platform's future innovations.

**What’s your favorite app idea you’ve always wanted to see built?**

The app I'm building, HOPSCOTCH, an innovative app that aims to extend the concept of social coding events to various hobbies.

Have a question? Reach out to us on [Discord](https://www.convex.dev/community)
, we’d love to talk.

Champions are
-------------

*   Active participants in the Discord community
*   Creators of Convex apps or content that features Convex
*   Active users that provide product feedback
*   Ambassadors of the product, spreading the word when relevant

Champions perks
---------------

*   Early previews to new features
*   AMAs with leadership
*   Exclusive Swag
*   Sponsored trips to conferences
*   Opportunity to give product feedback
*   Complimentary Professional Plan for one team\*
*   Private Discord connecting all champions

\* The Complimentary Professional Plan is available for one team at $0/month with support for up to five team members. The plan is non-transferable and non-resellable, and includes only the built-in monthly resource limits of the Professional plan.​

How to join
-----------

Are you interested in joining the Convex Developer Champions Program? If you’re an active member of our [Discord community](https://www.convex.dev/community)
 we’d love to hear from you. Complete the application form and a member from the Convex team will follow-up.

[Apply here](https://www.convex.dev/champions/apply)

Get your app up and running in minutes

[Start building](https://www.convex.dev/start)

Convex for Startups

Startups can apply for up to 1 year free of Convex Professional, including no seat fees and 30% off usage-based fees up to $30k. Start and scale your company with Convex.

[Learn more](https://www.convex.dev/startups)

Convex for **Startups**
=======================

![](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2Fstartups%2Fhero-grid-small.svg&w=768&q=75)![](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2Fstartups%2Fhero-grid-large.svg&w=768&q=75)

Start and scale your company with Convex.

Startups can apply for up to 1 year free of Convex Professional.

[Apply to the program](https://www.convex.dev/startups#apply)

*   Launch in minutesStartups launch or migrate their apps with Convex to a type-safe, reactive database in minutes, without worrying about backend infrastructure or scaling.
    
*   ![Typescript](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Ftypescript.d2644122.svg&w=48&q=75)
    
    Ready for AIConvex is the best way for AI to generate and enforce TypeScript schemas for your backend, making it ideal for AI coding platforms and app builders.
    
*   Built to scaleGrow from 1 to millions of users with live updates, transactions, and scaling baked in with no cache layers or scaling hacks to manage.
    
*   Your backend teamMore than a database. Get the backend, infra, and guidance you need to go from MVP to ARR, backed by the team that scaled Dropbox and S3.
    

Startups that are building with Convex
--------------------------------------

Startups in the Convex for Startups program have participated in leading accelerators and incubators like Y Combinator (YC) and NEO, raised millions in venture funding, and are backed by prominent investors such as OpenAI Fund, Index Ventures, and Accel.

YC startups scale with Convex
-----------------------------

Convex has simplified our backend operations, allowing our team to focus on delivering value to our customers. Its reliable transactions help us develop complex features quickly and reliably, tightening our feedback loop. Convex really helps us put functionality and user experience first.

Punn KamCo-founderConduit \- YC W24

[![Y Combinator](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fyc.f61c5efc.svg&w=96&q=75)](https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/conduit-ai)

[Read case study](https://www.convex.dev/case-studies/conduit)

Convex was a no brainer for us. We needed a backend that could scale to millions quickly and could enable real-time experiences between two people. This helped Candle create many magic moments and connect people all across the world

Parth ChopraCo-Founder and CTOCandle \- YC F24

[![Y Combinator](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fyc.f61c5efc.svg&w=96&q=75)](https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/candle)

With Convex, the majority of our backend and multiplayer complexities vanish. We can spin up isolated databases effortlessly for our users, cutting their dev time dramatically. I personally went from idea to four App Store launches in a single weekend.

Ayomide OmolewaCo-foundera0.dev \- YC W25

[![Y Combinator](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fyc.f61c5efc.svg&w=96&q=75)](https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/a0-dev)

Convex has been a game-changer for VibeFlow. We honestly love Convex components because they make it incredibly easy to add integrations into our apps. With very little code, Convex makes writing backends straightforward and removes so much complexity and backend headaches. The community is also genuinely supportive and always ready to help.

Alessia PaccagnellaCo-founderVibeFlow \- YC S25

[![Y Combinator](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fyc.f61c5efc.svg&w=96&q=75)](https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/vibeflow)

Empowering Startups
-------------------

We support early-stage startups with the infrastructure and resources they need to build scalable apps. Eligible teams can get up to 1 year free of Convex Professional, including no seat fees and 30% off usage-based fees up to $30k.

[Apply to join](https://www.convex.dev/startups/apply)

Complete a short form. We review applications continuously on a rolling 30-day basis.

![](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2Fstartups%2Feligibility-tunnel.svg&w=1024&q=75)

Who qualifies?
--------------

*   Early-stage startups
    
    This program is designed for startups at Series A or earlier stages, with fewer than 20 employees.
    
    The startup must be less than 3 years old.
    
*   Company profile
    
    Startups must have a valid company profile on LinkedIn, Crunchbase, PitchBook, or another recognized deal flow platform that is directly linked to the company.
    
    Include links to social proof of traction or articles announcing funding.
    
*   Funding
    
    Pre-seed to Series A companies that have raised less than $3 million.
    
*   Website
    
    Startups with a live, functioning website or product in the MVP stage.
    

![](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2Fstartups%2Fpartners-flair.svg&w=384&q=75)

Join our partner network for accelerators, VCs, and startup hubs
----------------------------------------------------------------

By becoming a program partner, you can grant your startup portfolio complimentary access to Convex, along with additional perks.

The database designed to be generated
=====================================

Prompt to create a full-stack app with [Convex Chef](https://chef.convex.dev/)

[Try Convex with\
\
![Convex Chef](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fchef-isolated.a1a595d9.svg&w=256&q=75)](https://chef.convex.dev/)

![](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fwave.0fe17fdb.svg&w=1536&q=75)

Rules & Prompts for AI
----------------------

Get the best results from AI by adding rules to your favorite AI IDEs like Cursor and Windsurf.

[Download Rules](https://docs.convex.dev/ai/#convex-ai-rules)

Building an AI Coding Platform?
-------------------------------

Convex is the best way for AI to generate the database and backend for your AI coding platform. Entirely in code.

[Learn more](https://www.convex.dev/ai-platforms)

Build AI Apps with ease
-----------------------

Convex Components make it easy to build intelligent, real-time features.

[### Workflow\
\
Simplifies long-running logic with durable execution\
\
Learn more →](https://www.convex.dev/components/workflow)
[### AI Agent\
\
Structures your AI flows with built-in memory and vector search\
\
Learn more →](https://www.convex.dev/components/agent)
[### Persistent Text Streaming\
\
Powers real-time chat with efficient storage\
\
Learn more →](https://www.convex.dev/components/persistent-text-streaming)

Why choose Convex for your next AI or AI generated app?
-------------------------------------------------------

### Thoroughly evaluated rules

Convex AI rules are thoroughly evaluated using [convex-evals](https://github.com/get-convex/convex-evals)
. You can see the results of the evaluation in the [LLM Leaderboard](https://www.convex.dev/llm-leaderboard)
. We've also created [full-stack benchmarks](https://stack.convex.dev/introducing-fullstack-bench)
 for AI coding to see that Convex outperforms other platforms.

### Queries are Code

Your database queries are pure TypeScript functions with end-to-end type safety and IDE support. This means AI can generate database code using the large training set of TypeScript code without switching to SQL.

### Automatic Reactivity

The reactive system automatically tracks data dependencies and updates your UI. AI doesn't need to manually manage subscriptions, WebSocket connections, or complex state synchronization—Convex handles all of this automatically.

### Transactional Guarantees

All functions that call the database are run in transactions. This makes it impossible for AI to write code that could corrupt your data or leave your app in an inconsistent state.

Get your app up and running in minutes

Podcast
=======

”Databased” is the essential podcast for web developers and technology leaders navigating the complexities of building scalable, high-performance applications in the modern startup landscape.

![Databased podcast cover image](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fcover.6a1cfb3f.png&w=1536&q=75)

[![YouTube icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fyoutube.646087f2.svg&w=64&q=75)YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6xox9MGzplFVl1hDmRyDAw6z3M81HUsv)
[![Apple Podcasts icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fapple.af24f74a.svg&w=64&q=75)Apple Podcasts](https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/the-databased-podcast/id1753798315)
[![Spotify icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fspotify.0bafb3e2.svg&w=64&q=75)Spotify](https://open.spotify.com/show/3UcRoUW5GKXEcA0hJc9hp6?nd=1&dlsi=6943d7dc94674a50)
[![RSS icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Frss.87514317.svg&w=64&q=75)RSS Feed](https://feed.podbean.com/databased/feed.xml)

Hosts Tom Redman, Jamie Turner, and James Cowling, known for their work building billion-dollar backend systems at Dropbox, dive deep into the world of databases, infrastructure, and edge computing.

Each episode challenges conventional wisdom and explores the realities of crafting applications that can withstand the demands of today’s users. Through expert insights, thought-provoking discussions, and entertaining segments, ”Databased” provides clarity amidst the hype, helping you make informed decisions about your technology stack.

Join us as we explore how to build and ship applications faster with a smaller team.

Components
==========

Independent, modular, TypeScript building blocks for your backend.

![Components](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2FcomponentsIcon.a64ca8d7.svg&w=48&q=75)

[AI](https://www.convex.dev/components#ai)
[Backend](https://www.convex.dev/components#backend)
[Database](https://www.convex.dev/components#database)
[Durable Functions](https://www.convex.dev/components#durable-functions)
[Integrations](https://www.convex.dev/components#integrations)
[Payments](https://www.convex.dev/components#payments)

*   ![Extended feature set](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Ffeature-extended.0ebaf1be.svg&w=96&q=75)
    
    Extended feature setInstantly add Convex-native features to your application without reaching for a third-party service.
    
*   ![Neatly packaged](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Ffeature-packaged.fa682ef8.svg&w=96&q=75)
    
    Neatly packagedExperience elegant installation DX with a clean npm package and well-documented API.
    
*   ![Best practices](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Ffeature-practices.5f51bf3f.svg&w=96&q=75)
    
    Best practicesLean on our curated backend and database best practices to confidently build scalable systems.
    
*   ![Sandboxed data & code](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Ffeature-sandboxed.02e63b58.svg&w=96&q=75)
    
    Sandboxed data & codeYour new feature data is automatically encapsulated in a sandbox and can only interact with explicitly defined APIs in your backend.
    

![](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2FiconChevron.72c5cd33.svg&w=48&q=75)

Durable Functions
-----------------

Long-running and resilient background task components.

Frequently Asked Questions
--------------------------

### How are components sandboxed?

The Convex runtime ensures that the component tables don't get mixed in with your app's tables. It also ensures that when the component runs its own Convex functions, they are not allowed to access your app's tables without explicit API calls.

### What projects should use Components?

If you see a feature listed above, feel free to use it. Check each component's documentation for further guidance.

### Can I build my own?

We'd love for you to build your own components to share and list on this page, and even make custom components specific to your project. We are still working on ironing out the authoring apis and will open that up soon.

### Do components cost money to use?

Components are open source and are just code and data in your existing backend. They incur relevant usage charges based on how they are implemented and used.

### Where can I learn more?

Visit the [Components documentation](https://docs.convex.dev/components)
 to learn about how Components work, their data isolation model, and how they help you build features faster.

Component Authoring Challenge

Build a reusable component for developers to drop into their projects. Win prizes. Get featured.

[Learn more](https://www.convex.dev/components/challenge)

Product Hunt / Hacker News Starter CloneA real-time app showcase directory built with Convex, Clerk, and Tailwind. The Vibe Apps starter is an open-source directory for sharing and browsing Vibe Coding projects. Includes real-time sync, full-text search, voting, comments, and admin tools for moderation, user management, and custom form handling. Features: • App submissions with screenshots, tags, and social links • Grid, list, and trending views with filters and sort options • Upvotes, comments (Markdown + threads), verified users • Admin panel for managing users, content, and forms • Built with React, Convex, Tailwind, Clerk, and Radix UI\
\
![Convex icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2F51662301-1a43-400c-8052-f2375a185e56&w=1536&q=75 "Convex")![Clerk icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Fd612bd25-2561-4999-9c33-c4ea9a42f91b&w=1536&q=75 "Clerk")![React icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Fe9a52db4-a84c-458a-a158-752d809b7040&w=1536&q=75 "React")](https://www.convex.dev/templates/product-hunt-digg-hacknews-clone)

[![Next.js + Convex AI Chat Starter v.0.1 hero image](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Fcb996dec-936d-4eef-9273-9d665432616a&w=1536&q=75)\
\
Next.js + Convex AI Chat Starter v.0.1\# Next.js + Convex AI Chat Starter A real-time AI chat application built with Next.js, Convex, Tailwind CSS, and OpenAI. ## 🛠 Tech Stack - Next.js - Convex - Tailwind CSS - OpenAI API - Vercel AI SDK (\`ai\` package) - react-textarea-autosize - TypeScript ## ✨ Features - Real-time chat interface using Convex and the Vercel AI SDK (\`useChat\`) for data synchronization and state management - AI responses powered by OpenAI, generated asynchronously in the background using Convex Actions to keep the UI responsive (supports multiple models) - Persistent storage of conversation history and chat sessions in the Convex database - Ability to clear the current chat session - Management of AI model preferences - Chat message archival functionality - User input handling with automatic textarea resizing - Responsive UI styled with Tailwind CSS and Shadcn/ui components - Toast notifications for user feedback\
\
![OpenAI icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fts10onj4%2Fproduction%2F446efd5641ab9946dd17e1922d18200338a9497d-225x225.png&w=1536&q=75 "OpenAI")![Convex icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2F51662301-1a43-400c-8052-f2375a185e56&w=1536&q=75 "Convex")![Next.js icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Faf922bb1-8148-4c3e-b62d-36120a5df3a5&w=1536&q=75 "Next.js")![Tailwind icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Fa06d631e-232f-421e-a1b8-503803a0d721&w=1536&q=75 "Tailwind")](https://www.convex.dev/templates/nextjs-ai-chat-template-convex)

\
CronvexSend http requests on a periodic schedule.\
\
![Convex icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2F51662301-1a43-400c-8052-f2375a185e56&w=1536&q=75 "Convex")](https://www.convex.dev/templates/open-source-cronvex)

[![Convex Auth hero image](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Faccea0e7-7df3-4dce-a965-203ecec1a8c3&w=1536&q=75)\
\
Convex AuthConvex Auth is a library for implementing authentication directly within your Convex backend.\
\
![Convex icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2F51662301-1a43-400c-8052-f2375a185e56&w=1536&q=75 "Convex")![Resend icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2F2a6ac19e-34e2-4f5d-82ae-24d21914a144&w=1536&q=75 "Resend")![Next.js icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Faf922bb1-8148-4c3e-b62d-36120a5df3a5&w=1536&q=75 "Next.js")](https://www.convex.dev/templates/convex-auth)

[![Vite and React Starter App on Convex Self Hosted hero image](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Fb51ad304-23eb-4d85-9d25-1c0a2323fe2f&w=1536&q=75)\
\
Vite and React Starter App on Convex Self HostedThe convex-react-keycloak-monorepo is a full-stack application template that integrates modern technologies to streamline development: - Frontend: Utilizes Vite with React and Tailwind CSS 4.0 for a responsive and efficient user interface. - Backend: Employs a self-hosted Convex server with PostgreSQL to manage data and server-side logic. - Authentication: Implements OAuth 2.0 authentication using Keycloak, ensuring secure user management. The project is organized as a monorepo, separating the frontend and backend components for clarity. A Docker Compose setup enables seamless deployment and development, allowing all services to run concurrently with minimal configuration. This template serves as a solid foundation for building scalable applications with modern web technologies.\
\
![Vite icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2F2034edc9-7ad9-46d0-a30d-7aa5d7ba4c74&w=1536&q=75 "Vite")![React icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Fe9a52db4-a84c-458a-a158-752d809b7040&w=1536&q=75 "React")![Convex icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2F51662301-1a43-400c-8052-f2375a185e56&w=1536&q=75 "Convex")](https://www.convex.dev/templates/convex-react-keycloak-monorepo)

[![AI Chat using OpenAI's Assistants API hero image](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fget-convex%2Fconvex-ai-chat-openai%2Fraw%2Fmain%2Fscreenshot.png&w=1536&q=75)\
\
AI Chat using OpenAI's Assistants APIAI chat with context retrieval using OpenAI Assistants API. This app demonstrates how you can add a chat bot to an existing website, powered by Convex and OpenAI's Assistants API. This is part of a series on implementing RAG multiple ways. Read the full article here, the LangChain version here, or learn how to build a custom implementation with Convex Vector Search here. See other articles on AI here. Overview: The chat is trigged by a button in App.tsx The chat frontend is all in src/aiChat An example of web scraping is in convex/ingest The assistant initialization and uploading of data is in convex/init.ts The public endpoints for the backend are in convex/messages.ts The answering logic is in convex/serve.ts\
\
![OpenAI icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fts10onj4%2Fproduction%2F446efd5641ab9946dd17e1922d18200338a9497d-225x225.png&w=1536&q=75 "OpenAI")![Tailwind icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Fa06d631e-232f-421e-a1b8-503803a0d721&w=1536&q=75 "Tailwind")](https://www.convex.dev/templates/ai-chat-openai)

[![Stripe Starter hero image](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fts10onj4%2Fproduction%2F97b28bec28caa96cbf88c99ff0355cbe22c4e6af-2400x1256.png%3Ffit%3Dcrop%26w%3D1200%26h%3D675&w=1536&q=75)\
\
Stripe StarterThis example app demonstrates how to integrate Stripe, the payments platform, with Convex, the backend application platform. We keep track of payments in Convex and fulfill orders when they're confirmed by Stripe. You can test the payment flow end to end using Stripe's test card numbersTo test the payments flow, follow these steps: Sign up for Stripe for free at https://stripe.com/ Install the stripe CLI RunCopy the "Your webhook signing secret" from the output of the listen command, and set it as STRIPE\_WEBHOOKS\_SECRET environment variable on your Convex dashboard Copy your test secret API key from the code example on https://stripe.com/docs/checkout/quickstart and set it as STRIPE\_KEY environment variable on your Convex dashboard You can then use the test credit card details to go through the payment flow, see https://stripe.com/docs/checkout/quickstart#testing\
\
![Stripe icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fts10onj4%2Fproduction%2F720c153a0ec04caea201987f8294d4c5f491a5e6-800x800.svg%3Fdpr%3D2%26fm%3Dpng&w=1536&q=75 "Stripe")](https://www.convex.dev/templates/stripe)

[![Embeddings with OpenAI and Pinecone hero image](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fts10onj4%2Fproduction%2Fa9c536f3f083b653502418b1dda6b41c68095b04-1200x638.png%3Ffit%3Dcrop%26w%3D1200%26h%3D675&w=1536&q=75)\
\
Embeddings with OpenAI and PineconeThis example demonstrates a fullstack application built with Convex, React, Next.js, and Auth0. The app is a very simple Asana-style task management app that allows users to create, view, and manage tasks. The app features: Basic Authentication & Authorization, implemented with Auth0 and Convex Task creation, where each task may have: Owner Title Description Status (New | In Progress | Done | Cancelled) Comments File attachments (from a fixed collection to prevent abuse) Task management, allowing users/task owner to: Add a comment Change status (owner only) Reassign ownership (owner only) Filter tasks by ticket status or owner Search tasks by title, description, or comments Infinite-scroll pagination to support very large task collections\
\
![Pinecone icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Fa42b871b-994e-475b-b451-703851ebd32f&w=1536&q=75 "Pinecone")![OpenAI icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fts10onj4%2Fproduction%2F446efd5641ab9946dd17e1922d18200338a9497d-225x225.png&w=1536&q=75 "OpenAI")![Tailwind icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Fa06d631e-232f-421e-a1b8-503803a0d721&w=1536&q=75 "Tailwind")![RewindUI icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2F95b90d86-f303-45f7-ba98-a41c8e1ce7cf&w=1536&q=75 "RewindUI")](https://www.convex.dev/templates/embeddings)

Fullstack: Project MGMT ExampleThis example demonstrates a fullstack application built with Convex, React, Next.js, and Auth0. The app is a very simple Asana-style task management app that allows users to create, view, and manage tasks. The app features: Basic Authentication & Authorization, implemented with Auth0 and Convex Task creation, where each task may have: Owner Title Description Status (New | In Progress | Done | Cancelled) Comments File attachments (from a fixed collection to prevent abuse) Task management, allowing users/task owner to: Add a comment Change status (owner only) Reassign ownership (owner only) Filter tasks by ticket status or owner Search tasks by title, description, or comments Infinite-scroll pagination to support very large task collections\
\
![Next.js icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Faf922bb1-8148-4c3e-b62d-36120a5df3a5&w=1536&q=75 "Next.js")](https://www.convex.dev/templates/fullstack)

\
Using Typeform with Convex ExampleThis is an example app using Typeform as a way to get data into a Convex project! The frontend (in /app) is custom to my specific example app, a dog-spotting "social media" app using a Typeform to collect the dogs users have seen. It's here for developing the scripts and Convex functions for Typeform integration, but isn't itself reusable since it's tightly coupled to my specific example Typeform.\
\

AI Agent
========

[![get-convex's avatar](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Favatars.githubusercontent.com%2Fu%2F81530787%3Fv%3D4&w=96&q=75)\
\
get-convex/agent\
\
View repo](https://github.com/get-convex/agent)
[![GitHub logo](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fnpm.bd053a67.svg&w=48&q=75)View package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@convex-dev/agent)

### Category

[AI](https://www.convex.dev/components#ai "Show all components in "AI" category")

![AI Agent hero image](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fagent.d9d5c243.png&w=1536&q=75)

    npm install @convex-dev/agent

AI Agents, built on Convex. [Check out the docs here](https://docs.convex.dev/agents)
.

The Agent component is a core building block for building AI agents. It manages threads and messages, around which you Agents can cooperate in static or dynamic workflows.

*   [Agents](https://docs.convex.dev/agents/agent-usage)
     provide an abstraction for using LLMs to represent units of use-case-specific prompting with associated models, prompts, [Tool Calls](https://docs.convex.dev/agents/tools)
    , and behavior in relation to other Agents, functions, APIs, and more.
*   [Threads](https://docs.convex.dev/agents/threads)
     persist [messages](https://docs.convex.dev/agents/messages)
     and can be shared by multiple users and agents (including [human agents](https://docs.convex.dev/agents/human-agents)
    ).
*   Streaming text and objects using deltas over websockets so all clients stay in sync efficiently, without http streaming. Enables streaming from async functions.
*   [Conversation context](https://docs.convex.dev/agents/context)
     is automatically included in each LLM call, including built-in hybrid vector/text search for messages in the thread and opt-in search for messages from other threads (for the same specified user).
*   [RAG](https://docs.convex.dev/agents/rag)
     techniques are supported for prompt augmentation from other sources, either up front in the prompt or as tool calls. Integrates with the [RAG Component](https://www.convex.dev/components/rag)
    , or DIY.
*   [Workflows](https://docs.convex.dev/agents/workflows)
     allow building multi-step operations that can span agents, users, durably and reliably.
*   [Files](https://docs.convex.dev/agents/files)
     are supported in thread history with automatic saving to [file storage](https://docs.convex.dev/file-storage)
     and ref-counting.
*   [Debugging](https://docs.convex.dev/agents/debugging)
     is enabled by callbacks, the [agent playground](https://docs.convex.dev/agents/playground)
     where you can inspect all metadata and iterate on prompts and context settings, and inspection in the dashboard.
*   [Usage tracking](https://docs.convex.dev/agents/usage-tracking)
     is easy to set up, enabling usage attribution per-provider, per-model, per-user, per-agent, for billing & more.
*   [Rate limiting](https://docs.convex.dev/agents/rate-limiting)
    , powered by the [Rate Limiter Component](https://www.convex.dev/components/rate-limiter)
    , helps control the rate at which users can interact with agents and keep you from exceeding your LLM provider's limits.

[Read the associated Stack post here](https://stack.convex.dev/ai-agents)
.

[![Powerful AI Apps Made Easy with the Agent Component](https://thumbs.video-to-markdown.com/b323ac24.jpg)](https://youtu.be/tUKMPUlOCHY)
 **Read the [docs](https://docs.convex.dev/agents)
 for more details.**

Play with the example:

    git clone https://github.com/get-convex/agent.git
    cd agent
    npm run setup
    npm run dev

Found a bug? Feature request? [File it here](https://github.com/get-convex/agent/issues)
.

Get your app up and running in minutes

[Start building](https://www.convex.dev/start)

Convex for  
AI Coding Platforms
================================

LLM development of fullstack applications on Convex produces more accurate results with fewer interventions than any other backend platform

[Get started](https://docs.convex.dev/platform-apis)

![](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fwave.6297d022.svg&w=1536&q=75)

Management API
--------------

Create, configure, and manage Convex projects on your own account through APIs.

For end-to-end app building platforms like Replit that want to handle everything for their users. Manage the Convex backend for your users completely behind the scenes. Users don't need to know they're using Convex.

[Read the docs to get started](https://docs.convex.dev/platform-apis)
 with Convex Platform APIs.

OAuth Applications
------------------

Allow users to authorize your app to access their own Convex account and projects.

For app building platforms like [Chef](https://chef.convex.dev/)
 that want users to bring their own Convex account and projects.

[Visit the Convex dashboard](https://dashboard.convex.dev/team/settings/applications/oauth-apps)
 to register your app and start building.

Why Choose Convex?
------------------

*   **Code-First Configuration:** Set up entire backends through code alone. No UI intervention needed for configuring auth, setting environment variables, or code deployment.
*   **AI-Optimized Rules:** Leverage Convex's [thoroughly evaluated rules](https://docs.convex.dev/ai)
     to ensure your AI generates high-quality, correct code.
*   **Embeded Convex Dashboard:** Let your users check their database, update environment variables, and run cloud functions directly from the UI.
*   **More free deployments:** Customers get up to 40 free deployments. Your users can build and iterate on multiple projects without cost concerns, and each project has separate development and production deployments.
*   **Ready-to-Use Templates:** Start with optimized [templates](https://www.convex.dev/templates)
     to avoid token usage on common setups.
*   **TypeScript Schema Enforcement:** Provide better guidance to AI Code Generation with strictly typed schemas.

[![Conduit logo](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Flogo.33a4254e.svg&w=640&q=75)](https://www.conduit.ai/)

Idea to Product in 3 Weeks
==========================

How Conduit built on Convex from the ground up
----------------------------------------------

[Start building](https://www.convex.dev/start)

![Timeline of Conduit's journey with Convex](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fconduit-diagram-vertical.73ba8d83.svg&w=1024&q=75)![Timeline of Conduit's journey with Convex](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fconduit-diagram.2a8dbf0f.svg&w=1536&q=75)

1

Learning about Convex

Conduit's cofounder Punn met our team at a hackathon, where he used our backend to build his projects for that day. He noted that it was particularly easy to spin apps up, prototype features, and reorganize the codebase quickly if the project specifications changed. From that day forward, whenever he'd need persistence or intermediate storage, he would look to Convex. Before Conduit, Punn built out a few side projects successfully with Convex, where he learned useful workflows and best practices.

2

Building the MVP

Punn then met his cofounder, Cole, at a Real Estate conference, who had Airbnb rentals that required too much management work. Punn and his cofounder started hacking away at a project to improve the Real Estate management experience by leveraging AI chat models for responding to guests. After validating the use case with some initial users, Punn continued the pattern of using Convex for his backend, and started hacking on the project. While developing Conduit, he noted that Convex enabled him to leave his schema flexible. With a lot of archaic data models in the real estate industry, reconciling modern and older models required constantly adding columns and changing types. The MVP was fully delivered within 3 weeks of starting the project.

3

Scaling the product

After developing the MVP, there was still a lot of outstanding work optimizing the user experience and application performance. Punn made particularly good use of Convex’s scheduler to manage asynchronous processes. Before using Convex’s scheduler, he would spin up Amazon SQS and have to do manual queue management, leading to queue congestion if not optimized. With the small team size, minimal overhead in managing infrastructure was essential to focus on the core goal: developing the product for customers. He moved all LLM calls, automated messages, and automated workflows to the Convex scheduler, leveraging Convex Actions to move external API calls out of the transaction path.

4

Building out features

The Conduit team has continued to build out complex features on Convex, adding VOIP support directly in their application to allow for direct phone calls. Along with a native task/workflow management system, upsells for guests, and updates to the core messaging tool, they were able to effectively prototype, implement, test, and deploy these features directly on Convex.

Get your app up and running in minutes

[Start building](https://www.convex.dev/start)

Apply to be a Convex Champion!
==============================

Champions use Convex to build fast, reliable, and dynamic apps. They support our thriving community through product feedback, design suggestions, and content.

Personal Information

Full name\*

Email address\*

Please use the same email address you use for Convex.

Location\*

Please provide the city, state/province, and country where you reside.

Discord username\*

The username you use for the Convex Community Discord server.

LinkedIn profile

For example, if your profile is https://www.linkedin.com/in/jane456/ use 'jane456'.

Convex Experience

Convex team slug\*

This is to confirm you are familiar with Convex. Your team slug is only for account lookup and can be found in the Convex dashboard under 'Team Settings'.

Are you currently on the Convex Professional Plan?\*

Select an option...YesNo

How many people on your team use Convex?\*

Select a team size1-4950-99100-199200-499500-9991000+

How did you learn about Convex?\*

Champions Program

What most excites you about joining the Champions program?\*

Select an option...Meeting other ChampionsEarly previews of new featuresExclusive AMAs with the Convex teamChampions swagLetting the world know about Convex

Anything else you'd like to share with us?

Submit Application

By applying, you agree to you agree to abide by our [Community Guidelines](https://discord.com/channels/1019350475847499849/1019358583630069760/1181777069986824202)
.

React Starter Kit (RSK)
=======================

[![GitHub logo](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fgithub.2fd3e44b.svg&w=48&q=75)View Repo](https://github.com/michaelshimeles/react-starter-kit)
[View Live](https://www.reactstarter.xyz/)

### Built with

[![Tailwind icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Fa06d631e-232f-421e-a1b8-503803a0d721&w=1536&q=75 "Tailwind")Tailwind](https://tailwindcss.com/)
[![React icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Fe9a52db4-a84c-458a-a158-752d809b7040&w=1536&q=75 "React")React](https://react.dev/?utm_source=convextemplates)
[![Convex icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2F51662301-1a43-400c-8052-f2375a185e56&w=1536&q=75 "Convex")Convex](https://docs.convex.dev/auth/convex-auth?utm_source=convextemplates)

`npx create-convex@latest -t michaelshimeles/react-starter-kit`

A modern, production-ready SaaS starter template for building full-stack React applications using React Router v7, Convex, Clerk, and Polar.sh. Ready for Vercel deployment with built-in AI chat capabilities.

Features:

*   React Router v7 - Modern full-stack React framework with SSR
*   Hot Module Replacement (HMR) - Fast development experience
*   Asset bundling and optimization - Production-ready builds
*   Data loading and mutations - Built-in loader/action patterns
*   TypeScript by default - Type safety throughout
*   TailwindCSS v4 - Modern utility-first CSS
*   Authentication with Clerk - Complete user management
*   Subscription management with Polar.sh - Billing and payments
*   Real-time database with Convex - Serverless backend
*   AI Chat Integration - OpenAI-powered chat functionality
*   Interactive Dashboard - User management and analytics
*   Webhook handling - Payment and subscription events
*   Responsive Design - Mobile-first approach
*   Vercel Deployment Ready - One-click deployment

![React Starter Kit (RSK) hero image](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2F420470ec-8a9e-4682-8206-437fd9490a3f&w=1536&q=75)

![](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fflair.3fae67ce.svg&w=96&q=75)

Get your app up and running in minutes

Apply to Convex for Startups
============================

Startups can apply for up to 1 year free, including savings on usage-based fees up to $30k.

Contact details

First name\*

Last name\*

Work email\*

Company details

Company name\*

Website\*

Company LinkedIn

Startup pitch\*

Funding raised\*

Select a funding rangeNone$0 - $50,000$50,000 - $100,000$100,000 - $500,000$500,000 - $1M$1M - $10M$10M+

Ideal qualified startups are those that have raised seed funding or demonstrated proven traction.

Team size\*

Select team size...Just Me2-1011-2020-5050+Other

Are you currently using Convex for your backend?\*

Select an option...YesNoNot yet

Are you currently on the Convex Professional Plan?\*

Select an option...YesNo

Convex GitHub login email

Only needed if different from your work email above.

Convex Team Slug\*

Your Team Slug is only for account lookup and can be found in the Convex Dashboard by clicking on "Team Settings". Please enter "N/A" if not applicable.

Referral partner code

If you have a referral partner code, enter it here.

Convex Community Discord name

Please enter "N/A" if not applicable.

Where did you hear about this offer?\*

Do you have any traction, growth, social proof, or press links? Is there anything else you'd like to share with us to help us verify your startup and assess its potential?\*

Apply

By applying, you agree to our [Terms and Conditions](https://convex.link/term4startups)
 and confirm that you meet the Convex For Startups Program eligibility criteria for the offer.

[Back to Episodes](https://www.convex.dev/podcast#episodes)

What You Missed in 2024 in the World of Databases, with Prof. Andrew Pavlo
==========================================================================

January 24, 2025 • 2816

![](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpbcdn1.podbean.com%2Fimglogo%2Fep-logo%2Fpbblog18936460%2FCONVEX-Databased-Pod_1500x1500px_Episodes_B_2_26ptxi.jpg&w=1024&q=75)

In this episode of Databased, James speaks with [Andrew Pavlo](https://scholars.cmu.edu/312-andrew-pavlo)
, Associate Professor of Computer Science at [Carnegie Mellon University](https://www.cmu.edu/)
, about the dramatic shifts reshaping the database industry in 2024. The conversation explores how Redis's controversial licensing changes sparked unprecedented community backlash and the fierce battle between Snowflake and Databricks that culminated in a $2 billion acquisition.

Andy also goes into modern testing approaches, particularly deterministic simulation, and why database wars now extend beyond performance metrics. The conversation also touches on the evolution of database education, the rising dominance of Postgres, and how modern testing approaches are reshaping reliability expectations in database development and deployment.

Through engaging stories and technical insights, Andrew illuminates the strategic moves and technological innovations driving the database industry forward, while offering practical wisdom for navigating these changes in real-world applications and system design.

Key Topics Discussed:

*   Analyzing the implications of Redis's controversial licensing change and the community's unprecedented response through the Valkey fork initiative.
*   Examining the escalating rivalry between Snowflake and Databricks, culminating in a $2 billion acquisition battle over Tabular.
*   Exploring DuckDB's rising influence in analytics and its integration with traditional database systems through multiple Postgres extensions.
*   Evaluating Amazon's strategic positioning with Aurora DSQL and its impact on the distributed database landscape.
*   Investigating the trend of AI companies entering database technology through strategic acquisitions like OpenAI's Rockset purchase.
*   Assessing the evolution of database testing methodologies, particularly the rise of deterministic simulation approaches.
*   Analyzing the shift in database wars from performance metrics to platform capabilities and ecosystem control.
*   Examining Postgres's emergence as the default choice for operational databases and its influence on vendor strategies.
*   Understanding the challenges and opportunities in hybrid transactional-analytical processing (HTAP) database systems.

Why Most Tech Content Doesn't Work ft. Web Dev Cody
===================================================

January 28, 2025 • 2715

![](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpbcdn1.podbean.com%2Fimglogo%2Fep-logo%2Fpbblog18936460%2FCONVEX-Databased-Pod_1500x1500px_Episodes_B_2-03698fh.jpg&w=1024&q=75)

In this special episode of Databased, [Jamie](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jamwt/)
 speaks with [Web Dev Cody](https://webdevcody.com/)
, a prominent technical content creator with almost 250,000 YouTube subscribers, about the evolving landscape of developer education and content creation. 

They discuss the delicate balance between maintaining authenticity and managing sponsored content, the impact of AI tools on both development and teaching, and practical insights into building genuine connections with technical audiences. 

Through candid discussion and real-world examples, Cody also illuminates how content creators can adapt to new tools while staying true to their voice, and offers valuable perspectives on developer marketing in an increasingly AI-assisted world.

Key Topics Discussed:

*   Balancing full-time software development with creating educational content for a large technical audience while maintaining quality and authenticity.
*   The impact of AI coding assistants on both software development workflows and technical education approaches.
*   Effective approaches to developer marketing and building genuine connections with technical audiences through content.
*   Adapting content creation strategies to incorporate new technologies while maintaining educational value and audience engagement.
*   Managing sponsored content relationships while preserving authenticity and trust with technical audiences.
*   Developing efficient content creation workflows that enable consistent output alongside full-time development work.
*   Investigating the role of authenticity and personal voice in technical education and content creation.
*   How AI tools are reshaping both software development practices and educational content approaches.
*   The challenges and opportunities in marketing technical products to developer audiences.
*   Strategies for teaching complex technical concepts in an increasingly AI-assisted development landscape.

Get your app up and running in minutes

Convex TanStack SaaS Starter
============================

[![GitHub logo](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=%2F_next%2Fstatic%2Fmedia%2Fgithub.2fd3e44b.svg&w=48&q=75)View Repo](https://github.com/get-convex/convex-saas)
[View Live](https://convex-saas.netlify.app/)

### Built with

[![Tailwind icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Fa06d631e-232f-421e-a1b8-503803a0d721&w=1536&q=75 "Tailwind")Tailwind](https://tailwindcss.com/)
[![Stripe icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fts10onj4%2Fproduction%2F720c153a0ec04caea201987f8294d4c5f491a5e6-800x800.svg%3Fdpr%3D2%26fm%3Dpng&w=1536&q=75 "Stripe")Stripe](https://stripe.com/)
[![TanStack icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2Ff4e755f3-03e0-47d5-afef-c3f85d3ff97a&w=1536&q=75 "TanStack")TanStack](https://tanstack.com/?utm_source=convextemplates)
[![Resend icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2F2a6ac19e-34e2-4f5d-82ae-24d21914a144&w=1536&q=75 "Resend")Resend](https://resend.com/?utm_source=convextemplates)
[![shadcn icon](https://www.convex.dev/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpleasant-albatross-666.convex.cloud%2Fapi%2Fstorage%2F0d7aa319-0b61-4eee-98b6-cf0ed7c074a8&w=1536&q=75 "shadcn")shadcn](https://ui.shadcn.com/?utm_source=convextemplates)

### Tags

[Starter](https://www.convex.dev/templates?tags=Starter "Show all templates tagged "Starter"")

`npx create-convex@latest -t get-convex/convex-saas`

A production-ready Convex Stack for your next SaaS application with Stripe integration, TanStack, Resend, Tailwindcss, and shadcn.

Features provided out of the box:

*   Convex: A complete, reactive, typesafe backend with authentication and file storage.
*   Vite: Next-gen frontend tooling.
*   Stripe: Subscription plans, customer portal, and more.
*   Authentication: Email code and social logins.
*   TailwindCSS: Utility-first CSS framework.
*   ShadCN: Composable React components.
*   Easy Theming: Switch between light and dark modes with ease.
*   TanStack Router: Simple route definitions.
*   Resend: Email for developers.
*   React Email: Customizable emails with React.
*   Conform: Type-safe form validation based on web fundamentals.
*   File Uploads: Profile picture uploads with Convex.
*   I18N: Internationalization for your app.
*   TanStack Development Tools: Enhanced development experience.
*   Modern UI: Carefully crafted UI with a modern design system.
*   Custom Pages: Landing, onboarding, dashboard, and admin pages.
*   Responsive: Works on all devices, from mobile to desktop.
